description: Create a content slide with a sidebar-left layout comparing somatic versus germline gene editing in a two-column table format, plus a right-hand text panel on CRISPR concerns and promises.

header.headline: … such as Nobel Prize winning gene editing process CRISPR – Concerns remain mainly in areas of germline gene editing

rows: Somatic therapies target genes in specific types of cells (blood cells, for example).
Sperm, egg, or embryos are edited.
Changes affect only the treated individual.
Changes are copied into every cell.
Risks are limited to the treated person.
Risks may affect future generations and the broader gene pool.
Edits are not passed to the next generation.
Edits can be inherited by next generations.
No broad consensus; major ethical concerns remain.

sidebar.body: CRISPR is a Nobel Prize-winning gene editing process. While genetic editing holds significant promise, concerns remain mainly in germline gene editing, especially because changes can be inherited and raise unresolved ethical questions.
